stimulus: Indigo was a highly valued dye throughout the kingdoms of West Africa. Yoruba weavers in particular were famous for using this plant-based dye to color their fabrics a striking deep blue. ______blank the word "indigo" itself, some linguists claim, may have entered European languages through Yoruba trade routes.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "In fact" logically signals that the claim in this sentence—the word "indigo" possibly entering European languages through Yoruba trade routes—emphasizes and supports the previous claim that Yoruba weavers were famous for their indigo dye.

Choice A is incorrect because "on the contrary" illogically signals opposition. Choice B is incorrect because "lastly" illogically signals a final step in a sequence. Choice C is incorrect because "regardless" illogically signals contrast.
